code:
class ListNode:
    def __init__(self, x):
        self.val = x
        self.next = None

class Solution:
    def getIntersectionNode(self, headA: ListNode, headB: ListNode) -> ListNode:
        if not headA or not headB:
            return None

        pA = headA
        pB = headB

        while pA != pB:
            # Move both pointers one step
            # If a pointer reaches the end of its list, redirect it to the head of the other list.
            # This allows the pointers to traverse (listA + listB) or (listB + listA) nodes.
            # If they intersect, they will meet at the intersection point.
            # If they don't, they will both become None simultaneously after traversing
            # the total length of both lists, and the loop will terminate.
            pA = pA.next if pA else headB
            pB = pB.next if pB else headA
            
        # At this point, pA == pB.
        # This can be the intersection node, or None if both reached None simultaneously (no intersection).
        return pA
prompt: Intersection point of two linked lists bul.
